task_completion.description: The T1 and T1ce volumes are loaded into Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Data' module or 'Subject Hierarchy' lists volumes containing 't1' and 't1ce' (specifically BraTS2021_00000), or they are visible in the slice views.

task_completion.description: The 'Subtract Scalar Volumes' module is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The module panel title shows 'Subtract Scalar Volumes' or the agent is searching for 'Subtract' in the module finder.

task_completion.description: Input volumes are set correctly for subtraction (T1ce minus T1).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Subtract Scalar Volumes module, 'Input Volume 1' is set to the T1ce (post-contrast) volume and 'Input Volume 2' is set to the T1 (pre-contrast) volume.
The 'Output Volume' selector in the module shows 'EnhancementMap' or 'Create new volume: EnhancementMap'.

task_completion.description: The subtraction result is generated and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slice views show the new 'EnhancementMap' volume (often appearing mostly dark with specific bright enhancing regions), or 'EnhancementMap' appears in the Data list.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the result to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog is visible, 'EnhancementMap' is checked, and the directory is set to '~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/' (or similar valid path).

integrity.description: The agent uses the Slicer GUI modules rather than Python scripting or terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the 'Subtract Scalar Volumes' module panel, buttons, and dropdowns.

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the actual 3D Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface (slice views, module panel, toolbar) is clearly visible and active.

integrity.description: The agent generates the map rather than loading an existing one.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not simply load a file named 'enhancement_map.nii.gz' from the disk without performing the subtraction steps first.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The specific sample ID is BraTS2021_00000. The correct subtraction order is T1ce (post-contrast) minus T1 (pre-contrast). Input 1 must be the T1ce volume and Input 2 must be the T1 volume. The output file must be named 'enhancement_map.nii.gz'.
